behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure can be isolated to local classification/counting logic (rename event must map to exactly one bucket). REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess is required to reproduce miscounting if the input change records include a rename. Rejection gate: Integration/System were considered, but behavior text does not require real git rename detection mechanics, only correct categorization once a rename is present.
behaviors.test_description: Call the change-classification/counting function with a fixture containing one rename record and assert: renamed=1, added=0, deleted=0 for that record, and no double-counting.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: binary diff handling commonly fails at real git/subprocess/stdout decoding boundaries. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing parsed diff text can hide byte/encoding/exit-code issues that occur only with real binary content. Rejection gate: Integration was considered, but behavior explicitly ties to real diffs including binary files; that boundary is subprocess+filesystem.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, commit a binary file change, run the CLI as a subprocess against the repo, and assert exit code 0 plus report includes the binary-changed file in counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: unborn-HEAD behavior is a real git repository state and often surfaces via real command failures/messages. MOCK-HIDE: fakes may not reproduce exact no-commit repo semantics and subprocess exit behavior. Rejection gate: Integration was considered, but behavior is specifically about running on an actual repo state with no commits.
behaviors.test_description: Create a fresh git repo with no commits, execute the CLI subprocess, and assert successful run with an empty structured report (not an exception/error output).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/REAL-INFRA: this behavior validates end-to-end correctness against real repository history, requiring real git history traversal and counting over true repo state. MOCK-HIDE: mocked history providers can trivially mirror expected counts and miss integration defects in history extraction. Rejection gate: Integration was considered, but behavior text anchors correctness to the actual repository history, not a synthetic in-process history object.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a temp repo with known sequence of commits including add/delete/rename/modify, run CLI subprocess, and compare reported category counts to independently computed ground truth from the repo history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: staged vs unstaged is a real git index/working-tree boundary. MOCK-HIDE: stubs often flatten this distinction and miss failures in command selection (`--cached` vs working tree) and state reads. Rejection gate: Integration was considered, but behavior explicitly depends on real index/worktree semantics that are subprocess+filesystem coupled.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, produce equivalent change sets once staged and once unstaged, run CLI subprocess in both states, and assert identical structured stdout output.
